server.description: Defines a virtual server. Each server block can listen on different ports and respond to different domain names.

location.description: Sets configuration based on the request URI. Modifiers: = (exact), ~ (regex), ~* (case-insensitive regex), ^~ (prefix priority).

upstream.description: Defines a group of servers for load balancing. Supports round-robin, least_conn, ip_hash, and hash methods.

listen.description: Sets the address and port on which the server accepts requests. Can include ssl and http2 parameters.

server_name.description: Sets the virtual server names. Supports exact names, wildcard names (*.example.com), and regex names.

root.description: Sets the root directory for requests. The full path is formed by appending the URI to the root path.

proxy_pass.description: Passes the request to a proxied server. The URL can include protocol, server address, and optional URI.

proxy_set_header.description: Redefines or appends fields to the request header passed to the proxied server.

ssl_certificate.description: Specifies the PEM-format certificate file for the server. Can include intermediate certificates.

ssl_certificate_key.description: Specifies the PEM-format private key file for the server certificate.

try_files.description: Checks the existence of files in order, and uses the first found. Often used for SPA routing with =404 fallback.

rewrite.description: Rewrites the request URI using PCRE regex. Flags: last, break, redirect (302), permanent (301).

return.description: Stops processing and returns the specified code. Commonly used with 301/302 for redirects.

gzip.description: Enables or disables gzip compression of responses. Reduces transfer size at the cost of CPU.

access_log.description: Sets the path, format, and configuration for the access log.

error_log.description: Configures error logging. Levels: debug, info, notice, warn, error, crit, alert, emerg.

client_max_body_size.description: Sets the maximum allowed size of the client request body. Default is 1m. Set to 0 to disable checking.

add_header.description: Adds a custom header to the response. Use always to add header regardless of response code.

limit_req_zone.description: Defines a shared memory zone for rate limiting. Key is typically $binary_remote_addr.

expires.description: Sets the Expires header and Cache-Control max-age. Common values: 30d, 1h, max.

index.description: Defines files that will be used as an index. Checked in order, the first found is used.
